neighborhoods.name: Casco Viejo (Casco Antiguo)
neighborhoods.description: UNESCO World Heritage colonial quarter on a peninsula, with restored historic buildings, rooftop bars, and waterfront plazas
neighborhoods.character: Historic, walkable, gentrifying, vibrant nightlife and restaurant scene

housing.predominantType: Restored colonial apartments and modern loft conversions

neighborhoods.character_notes: Active gentrification ongoing. Mix of luxury and unrestored buildings. UNESCO status protects architecture. Can be noisy on weekends.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Panama City

neighborhoods.name: Punta Pacifica
neighborhoods.description: Modern high-rise district on a point jutting into the Pacific, home to luxury towers, Johns Hopkins-affiliated hospital, and Multiplaza mall
neighborhoods.character: Upscale modern, high-rise living, ocean views, international feel

housing.predominantType: Luxury high-rise condos with pools, gyms, and ocean views

neighborhoods.character_notes: Hospital Punta Pacifica (Johns Hopkins affiliate) is here. Multiplaza Pacific mall. Metro station nearby. Most popular expat high-rise area.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Panama City

neighborhoods.name: El Cangrejo
neighborhoods.description: Central residential neighborhood with a mix of mid-rise apartments, restaurants, and the Via Argentina pedestrian boulevard
neighborhoods.character: Middle-class residential, diverse, walkable main street, convenient

housing.predominantType: Mid-rise apartments and older condos, some with doorman

neighborhoods.character_notes: Via Argentina is the main walkable strip with cafes and shops. Close to metro. More affordable than Punta Pacifica. Diverse dining scene.

sources.title: Expatistan Cost of Living Panama City

neighborhoods.name: Clayton (Ciudad del Saber)
neighborhoods.description: Former US military base converted to a knowledge city with tree-lined streets, parks, and low-rise buildings surrounded by rainforest
neighborhoods.character: Green, quiet, campus-like, family-friendly, close to nature

housing.predominantType: Converted military housing, townhouses, and newer apartments

neighborhoods.character_notes: Adjacent to Parque Natural Metropolitano (rainforest within the city). Ciudad del Saber hosts NGOs and international orgs. Very green and quiet.

neighborhoods.name: Coronado Beach
neighborhoods.description: Beach community 80km west of Panama City, popular weekend retreat that has grown into a full-time expat community with amenities
neighborhoods.character: Beach resort community, expat-heavy, gated communities, relaxed pace

housing.predominantType: Houses and condos in gated beach communities with pools

neighborhoods.character_notes: Coronado Mall and Riba Smith supermarket. 90-minute drive to Panama City. Large English-speaking community. Golf courses. Car essential.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Panama City
